search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG75 linear growth assessment child height monitoring referral
site:dha.gov.ae short stature endocrine guideline UAE
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic endocrine tests, growth hormone or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Growth interpretation requires age, sex, serial standardized measurements and population charts.
No investigation, hormone, dose or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Children with linear-growth concern; NICE NG75 is principally a faltering-weight guideline and therefore provides only partial support.
setting_applicability: Primary or specialist paediatric growth review.
UAE_applicability: NICE assessment concepts require UAE growth-chart, paediatric, laboratory and endocrine referral adaptation.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG75 exact assessment and monitoring sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact paediatric growth-assessment section supports onset and change, associated features, relevant negatives, measured weight or BMI, focused assessment and clinician-reviewed investigations without diagnosing short stature or a cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies weight and height measurement, clinical/developmental/social assessment, detailed feeding or eating history, observation, and assessment-directed investigations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact monitoring and referral section supports patient or family concerns, clinician concern, entered plan, safety net and arranged follow-up without setting an interval or referral decision.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines growth monitoring and referral for underlying disorder, failure to respond, slow growth, rapid weight loss, severe undernutrition, safeguarding concern, or acute illness.

unresolved_source_gaps: Age, sex, height, centile, growth velocity, parental heights, puberty, birth history, nutrition, chronic disease, endocrine symptoms, bone age, laboratories, diagnosis and treatment remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
